label: strength
summary: For a strength goal, rep quality should prioritize repeatable setup, stable positions, and clear top and bottom landmarks.
coaching_cues: Keep the setup repeatable from rep to rep.
Stop the set when rep quality drops enough to change the movement pattern.

label: hypertrophy
summary: For a hypertrophy goal, consistency across reps and controlled range of motion matter more than rushing the set.
coaching_cues: Use a controlled tempo you can repeat.
Aim for similar depth and finish positions across the whole set.

label: endurance
summary: For an endurance goal, the key pattern to watch is whether technique drifts late in the set as fatigue builds.
coaching_cues: Track where rep quality starts to change.
Back off pace slightly if later reps become harder to control.

label: mobility
summary: For a mobility goal, range and control should be discussed conservatively and without forcing deeper positions than the evidence supports.
coaching_cues: Favor smooth controlled motion over speed.
Use consistent camera setup to compare range over time.

label: beginner_practice
summary: For beginner practice, feedback should stay simple, visible, and focused on one or two repeatable adjustments.
coaching_cues: Focus on one clear fix at a time.
Keep the camera angle consistent so the next set is easier to compare.
